Petition For Court Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Retention Of Jurisdiction To Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Sep-13-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Greer Smith appeared via zoom for and with Utopia Hammond (petitioner).
Andrew Dosa and Donna Beckham appeared via zoom for and with Bruce Smith (heir).

The Court ordered the Heggstad petition set for October 24, 2023 off calendar. Mrs. Greer stated that the petition that was filed was for pending litigation, that it's required by CCP 664.6. Petition for Court approval of settlement agreement is taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Wong. Not reported.

Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu appeared by zoom for and with Utopia Hammond by zoom.
Donna Echiverri-Beckham and Andrew Dosa appeared by zoom for and with Bruce Smith (beneficiary) by zoom.

Mr. Dosa asked the court to assist them to effectuate the settlement, and to order Carol Sullivan to step in and serve as successor trustee. The court informed the parties that they will need to file a petition to approve the settlement agreement, and file a petition for the appointment of a successor trustee. Mrs. Wu noted that the trust instrument governs how to appoint a successor trustee. The parties decided to work on this on their own. The Petition for an Order to Confirm Validity of Trust, etc. and the Petition to Invalidate Trust Based on Undue Influence, etc. are off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-07-2023 Continued To Feb-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Continued To Mar-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Gwen Wu, Esq. for and with Utopia Hammond (Petitioner), also via Zoom. Andrew Dosa, Esq. and Donna Eschiverri-Beckham, Esq, for Robert Bruce Smith (Petitioner).

The Court inquired about the status of trial. Mr. Dosa stated that they have resolved the matters, that a settlement has been reached after extensive mediation, that Ms. Hammond is to move which will require substantial funds, and that a new person will serve as successor trustee. The Court stated that counsel have a trial date today, that the Court did not receive the joint case management statement, and that the parties need to keep the Court informed. Ms. Wu stated that an ex parte to continue the trial date was filed but the clerk informed them that today was just a trial readiness date. The Court stated that counsel should proceed pursuant to the Court's case management order. Mr. Dosa stated that they have a signed stipulation by both parties to get the matter off the Court's calendar. The Court stated that this should have been presented before today noting that this is not the first time that it has talked to counsel about compliance with the CMC order. The Court stated that this Court doesn't approve settlements unless by petition. Counsel stated that there needs to be an order appointing the successor, that it also needs to address five or six accounts which are frozen, and that the successor will need access to those funds. The Court stated that a trustee appointment is by petition, that it will grant a brief continuance of the trial, that counsel need to get it together, and that it doesn't know if it can appoint a successor on an ex parte basis. Ms. Hammond stated that bank said the accounts were frozen and that she would need a form, that the accounts are not in the name of the trust, and that she is on one joint account. Brief discussions are held. The Court stated that counsel need to figure out how to get the funds/access the accounts which may either be by way of a small estate affidavit or a petition for letters in an estate matter. The Court noted that appointing a successor might be able to be done without a court order. Mr. Dosa stated that the settlement would be pursuant to CCP 664.6. The Court stated that counsel are to file a joint statement where things stand and get an ex parte together. Brief discussions regarding a continuance date are held.

The petition to invalidate the trust and the petition for an order to confirm validity of the trust are continued to February 7, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Today's trial date is continued to March 28, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
The status on mediation is off calendar.
Ms. Hammond noted that she is to move out by March 31st. The Court advised counsel to continue to work towards the settlement.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Based On Undue Influence, Elder Abuse, Lack Of Capapcity And Failure To Obtain Certificate Of Independent Review (Original Petition Was Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-22-305535 On July 28, 2022)
Off Calendar Dec-21-2022 Continued To Jan-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu, via Zoom, for Utopia Hammond, Petitioner; Donna Echiverri-Beckham, via Zoom, for and with Bruce Smith, Objector.

The court acknowledges the joint case management statement submitted by counsel and confirms that the matter did not settle via pro bono mediation. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to their respective petitions. The court queries whether Mr. Smith's petition is ready for litigation. Ms. Wu argues that presently, only her client's petition is ready for trial but advises that she is amenable to both petitions being considered at trial. The court notes the issues in setting both cases for trial and that it intends to avoid those issues via the case management order. The court points to its position that the burden of proof in Bruce Smith's petition has not been established. Ms. Echiverri-Beckham advises of her intent to submit evidence of fraud and undue influence. The court advises that the preceding is not within the petition nor the objection. Ms. Echiverri-Beckham advises that the preceding is mentioned in the joint case management statement and her statement is confirmed by the court. Ms. Wu reports on trial readiness and advises that the matter should be ready for trial as currently set in January 2023. In an effort to save judicial resources, the court confirms with Ms. Wu and Ms. Echiverri-Beckham on the record that they stipulate to a simultaneous trial of both Ms. Hammonds' Heggstad Petition and Mr. Smith's Petition to Invalidate Trust with the court advising that it is reserving the right to clarify that only Ms. Hammonds' petition will proceed to trial in January 2023. The court reminds counsel to review the Case Management Order section regarding the joint trial plan. Ms. Echiverri-Beckham advises that her client received notice that the real property may not be properly insured. Ms. Wu advises that this issue was discussed at mediation and therefore should not be discussed at this hearing. The court reminds Ms. Echiverri-Beckham that this Trust is not under court supervision and suggests that she file a formal petition to address the insurance issue.

After additional discussion, the Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar. The remaining subject matters are continued to Jan-10-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Aug-09-2022 Continued To Nov-01-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-09-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu, via Zoom, for Utopia Hammond, Petitioner; Donna Echiverri-Beckham, via Zoom, for Robert Smith, Objector.

Counsel report that they are willing to mediate and the court asks if they are amenable to participating in the pro bono mediation program. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Oct-21-2022. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Oct-26-2022. The Parties are to return on Nov-01-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Ms. Wu is instructed to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order and serve it on all parties who are entitled to notice. The court and Echiverri-Beckham discuss a related matter that has been inadvertently filed under a new case number. Ms. Echiverri-Beckham is instructed to file an ex parte petition to consolidate the matters, with a request that all future filings be made in this matter. The court and Ms. Wu briefly discuss statutory deadlines regarding the filing of a demurrer. The court advises Ms. Wu that she should take action accordingly.

After additional discussion, the Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar. The Second Amended Heggstad Petition, etc., is continued to Nov-01-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of Petition to Consolidate is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Court'S Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Donna Echiverri-Beckham, via Zoom, for Robert Smith, Respondent; Gwen Wu, via Zoom, for Utopia Hammond, Petitioner.

The court acknowledges the most recent joint case management statement and advises to focus on providing the court with the undisputed vs. disputed facts, and material facts, for the next joint case management statement. The court and counsel discuss the status of the matter. The court confirms with Ms. Echiverri-Beckham that the power of attorney signature date is Nov/Dec of 2019. The court confirms with counsel that they may be ready to participate in ADR by August. Ms. Wu points to several issues referenced by Ms. Echiverri-Beckham in the joint case management statement, are not currently before the court. The court confirms that if this case is sent to trial, the only petition being sent to trial is the Heggstad petition. The court advises counsel to meet and confer regarding the issue being sent to trial as both sides should be aware of the substance of the trial.

After additional discussion, the court's motion for sanctions and case management hearing are taken off calendar. The Second Amended Heggstad Petition is continued to Aug-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Continued To May-17-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu appeared by zoom for Utopia Hammond.
Donna Echiverri-Beckham appeared by zoom for and with Bruce Smith by zoom.

The court stated that it received and reviewed the joint status statement, that its understanding is that discovery is underway and that everyone has been served, and informed the parties that it will be issuing a case management order after today's hearing. The court also advised the parties to review the order carefully and calendar the dates that are in it, that it has very detailed information and dates that run from it, that it has dates for filing of joint case management statements as well as a joint trial plan, that it sets three case management hearings and a trial date, and that this is the order that will govern adjudication going forward of this petition. The court also advised the parties that the case management order will require them to provide a lot of information so they will have to invest time and submit comprehensive joint case management statements as required in the order. The parties are to monitor the court's website/register of actions and look out for the order to be downloaded. Ms. Wu is to serve a copy of the case management order on all parties who are entitled to service. The Petition for an Order to Confirm Validity of Trust, etc. is continued to May 17, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The first case management hearing is also set for the same date. Other hearing dates will be set pursuant to the dates that are reflected in the case management order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Dec-20-2021 Continued To Feb-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Nov-09-2021 Continued To Dec-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Buggy, via ZOOM, for Florence Utopia Hammond, Trustee; Andrew Dosa and Donna Echiverri-Beckham, via ZOOM, for Robert Smith, Objector.

The court reminds Mr. Buggy that this matter has been on calendar nine times and asks for an update regarding the number of heirs who have been served since the last calling of this matter. Mr. Buggy reports that five of the six remaining heirs have been served and that that there had been a delay caused by a clerical error but that the proof of service is ready to be filed. Mr. Buggy advises that a possible address has been found for Mr. Smith, the last heir to be served. The court advises that it will be granting one additional continuance but that the court will consider taking the matter off calendar if there appears to be inadequate service. Mr. Buggy advises that he will be filing a change of handling attorney in this matter as he will be leaving Open Door Legal soon.

After additional discussion, the Petition for An Order to Confirm Validity of Trust, etc., is continued to Dec-21-2021 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Sep-07-2021 Continued To Nov-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Buggy, via ZOOM, for Utopia Hammond, Petitioner; Andrew Dosa and Donna Echiverri-Beckham, via ZOOM, for Robert Smith, Respondent.

The court begins by confirming with Mr. Buggy that nine (9) of seventeen (17) heirs have been served. Mr. Buggy reports that there had been an issue with the company his firm uses to provide service but that the issue has now been resolved and that the company is waiting for a new hearing date, if granted by the court, to attempt service on the remaining heirs. The court advises that it will grant one additional continuance. Mr. Dosa nor Ms. Echiverri-Beckham object to the request for continuance.

The Petition for an Order to Confirm Validity of Trust, etc., is continued to 11/09/2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Jun-22-2021 Continued To Sep-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andre Dosa and Donna Echiverri-Beckham, via ZOOM, for Robert Smith, via ZOOM, Trustee; Kevin Buggy, via ZOOM, for Utopia Hammond, Petitioner.

Mr. Buggy is advised at the outset of notice issues to the heirs. Mr. Buggy reports that he will make certain to have the out-of-state heirs personally served and requests time to do so. Mr. Dosa states that he is authorized to accept service on behalf of his client and is amenable to a continuance. Ms. Echiverri-Beckham requests that any service to the office be made to require a signature of receipt due to office staff working remotely.

The Petition for Order to Confirm Validity of Trust, etc., is continued to September 7, 2021, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Neves. Not reported.
